codeReview.summary: The fix correctly preserves unsaved editor drafts across delete-cancel and stops newly opened repair flows from auto-selecting replacement identities, but it still fails to preserve auth-family intent for already-broken hosts during database migration.

issues.description: `MIGRATION_3_4` backfills `hosts.authKind` only from the currently linked identity kind and otherwise falls back to `PASSWORD`. Any key-auth host whose `identityId` was already nulled by the old delete flow before upgrade is therefore migrated as password-auth, so reopening repair after this fix still drifts into the wrong credential family for exactly the previously broken records this rerun is meant to recover. `core/data/src/androidTest/kotlin/io/github/jtsang4/aterm/core/data/FoundationRepositoriesInstrumentedTest.kt:505-514` even codifies that orphaned hosts migrate to `PASSWORD`, confirming the prior key-repair failure remains unresolved for existing data.

summary: Fail. I reviewed the mission docs, prior failed reviews, worker transcript skeleton, handoff, and commit `c0a39a4dcf900f956c4fec0051bf08afb7dd79cf`. The new code fixes draft preservation and explicit replacement selection for fresh repair flows, but the migration still converts already-orphaned hosts to password auth, so the original auth-family repair failure is not fully resolved for existing affected users.
